title: Air Fryer Duck Pancakes
summary: One of our all time favourite orders in a Chinese restaurant. This is a very non authentic way to make it with very few ingredients.

method.description: Coat the duck in the five-spice and pop half a lime in the cavity. Transfer to the air fryer and cook at 180°C for 1 hr.
After 20 mins of cooking, carefully tip some of the oil from the air fryer basket into a bowl for you to discard safely, brush the duck in some of the oil but don’t over do it.
Once done, allow the cooked duck to rest for 15 mins. Use a knife to cut the meat from the bone, then use two forks to roughly shred it up.
While the duck is resting, steam the pancakes (you can do this in the microwave), slice the spring onions lengthways and cut the cucumber into thin matchsticks.
Serve the pancakes alongside the shredded duck, spring onions, cucumber and hoisin sauce.

chefs.name: Nathan Anthony
chefs.summary: Meet Nathan Anthony, the Northern Irish home cook behind Bored of Lunch. During lockdown, he started his food blog to others to cook and eat healthier giving everyone something positive to focus on. Specialising in easy, budget-friendly recipes, Nathan’s platform boasts over 4 million online followers and subscribers. With four bestselling cookbooks already under his belt, Nathan’s journey with Bored of Lunch has been nothing short of remarkable. Join the BOL family and explore his delicious recipes across the website, cookbooks, and social media channels.
